title: Button Component
content:
## Button Component The `Button` component provides a customizable and accessible button element for the UI. ### Purpose This component simplifies the creation of buttons with various styles and sizes, leveraging `class-variance-authority` for efficient class management and `Radix UI's Slot` for composition. ### Key Exports * **`Button`**: The main React component for rendering buttons. It accepts standard button attributes and custom `variant` and `size` props, as well as an `asChild` prop for composition. * **`buttonVariants`**: A `class-variance-authority` function that generates CSS class strings based on the `variant` and `size` props. This is useful for applying styles consistently. ### Props The `Button` component extends `React.ButtonHTMLAttributes` and `VariantProps`. * **`className`**: (Optional) Additional CSS classes to apply to the button. * **`variant`**: (Optional) Controls the visual style of the button. Accepts one of: `default`, `destructive`, `outline`, `secondary`, `ghost`, `link`. * **`size`**: (Optional) Controls the size of the button. Accepts one of: `default`, `sm`, `lg`, `icon`. * **`asChild`**: (Optional, boolean, defaults to `false`) If `true`, the button will render as a `Slot` component from Radix UI, allowing for composition with other elements. ### Usage Example ```jsx import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; function MyComponent() { return (
); } ``` ### Variants * **`variant`**: * `default`: Primary action button. * `destructive`: For actions that delete or are harmful. * `outline`: Bordered button with transparent background. * `secondary`: Secondary action button. * `ghost`: Button with no background by default, appears on hover. * `link`: Underlined text link styled as a button. * **`size`**: * `default`: Standard button size. * `sm`: Small button. * `lg`: Large button. * `icon`: Icon-only button. ### Composability with `asChild` When `asChild` is set to `true`, the `Button` component renders a `Slot` component. This is useful for applying button styles to other elements, such as links or div elements, allowing for flexible UI construction.
